generator_prompt_injection:
REAL TEST-TAKER INSIGHTS (sourced from r/Sat, updated 2026-05-09):
- Coverage: Real test-takers report this exam covers breadth over depth. Stay at college 101 level on each topic.
- High-emphasis topics (per real students): algebra.
- Students struggle most with: sat prep, equation solving, depth under water. Generate proportionally more on these.
- Wording style: ~12% of items should use realistic exam-style indirection (NOT unfair ambiguity).
- Difficulty target: 15% easy / 50% medium / 35% hard. avg pass score 1288 — harder test.
- Authenticity bar: students compare us to Desmos, SAT class, practice tests. Match that register.

popup_tips.text: Students dont have to learn algebra, but they can just copy and paste the question on a graphing calculator and get the answer.
popup_tips.source_attribution: From a recent SAT MATH test-taker

popup_tips.text: Lets say for instance, there is a equation: 4 + 5x = 192 - 9x.
popup_tips.source_attribution: From a recent SAT MATH test-taker

popup_tips.text: Do you really HAVE to use a calculator to do a quick arithmetic question like 11 * 14?
popup_tips.source_attribution: From a recent SAT MATH test-taker

popup_tips.text: Students are not learning how to do math, but rather, learning how to use a graphing calculator.
